justification: All pass criteria met: FulfillmentWorker Kafka trace break clearly identified as CRITICAL; NotificationService and AuditService missing trace context both identified with specific interaction-level detail; consequence explicitly named (manual correlation across 3+ services, '5-minute investigation into a 45-minute one'); cross-service SLO gap identified with specific recommendation for order lifecycle metric; specific fix recommendations provided for each trace break (extract from Kafka headers, propagate X-Trace-ID on all outbound HTTP); verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough trace break analysis — mapped every interaction edge and correctly identified all three broken/risk paths
Consequence articulated concretely with operational impact (5-min vs 45-min investigation time)
Cross-service SLO gap identified with actionable metric recommendation (order_completed_duration_seconds)
Goes beyond rubric requirements with resilience assessment covering failure modes, retry/timeout/circuit breaker gaps
Correctly routes escalations to Coach K with clear reasoning
Appropriately conservative confidence level (55) given assumptions about unobserved configurations

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: FulfillmentWorker Kafka trace break clearly identified as root cause; NotificationService and AuditService missing trace context both identified with specific interaction mappings; consequence named explicitly (on-call engineer cannot trace full path, must manually correlate across disconnected trace trees); cross-service SLO gap identified with dedicated escalation; specific fix recommendations for each trace break (extract from Kafka headers, propagate X-Trace-ID on all HTTP calls, standardize on W3C Trace Context); verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough component interaction mapping — identifies all six integration points with correct status assessments
Clearly names the consequence: engineer must manually correlate by order ID, timestamps, and Kafka offsets across three disconnected trace trees
Cross-service SLO gap explicitly called out with dedicated escalation to Coach K
Specific fix recommendations per trace break, including W3C Trace Context standardization and OpenTelemetry LINK for async Kafka boundary
Goes beyond minimum requirements with resilience assessment, failure mode analysis, and detailed infrastructure change list
Honest confidence level (55%) with well-reasoned assumptions about what was not provided in the scenario

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: FulfillmentWorker Kafka trace break clearly identified; NotificationService and AuditService missing trace context both identified with specific broken edges; consequence named explicitly (manual timestamp-based correlation across three independently-traced services at 3am); cross-service SLO gap identified (no end-to-end order latency metric, no cross-service SLO tracking); specific fix recommendations for each trace break (FulfillmentWorker extract from Kafka headers, propagate X-Trace-ID to NotificationService and AuditService, OrderService propagate to AuditService); verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough component interaction mapping — identified all four broken edges, not just the primary Kafka break
Consequence articulation is vivid and operationally grounded ('painful, error-prone process at 3am')
Cross-service SLO gap called out with multiple specific examples (p99 order-to-fulfillment, end-to-end latency, error-rate correlation)
Fix recommendations are specific to each broken edge rather than generic
Goes beyond rubric requirements with resilience assessment covering failure modes, retry/timeout/circuit breaker gaps
Correctly identifies the cascading nature — FulfillmentWorker break propagates to NotificationService and AuditService downstream
